title: Sparse Multi-Modal Transformer with Masking for Alzheimer's Disease Classification
problem_statement: To address the limitations of existing multi-modal transformers for AD classification, which suffer from high computational cost and poor performance with missing data.

key_innovations: Cluster-based sparse attention mechanism to reduce computational cost.
Modality-wise masking strategy to improve generalization with incomplete data.
limitations_of_compared_model: Computational complexity scales quadratically with sequence length (O(n^2)).
Lacks explicit mechanisms to handle missing modalities.

results_and_performance.efficiency_improvement: 40.4% reduction in training energy consumption compared to 3MT.
